title: Seattle · Four Decades of Crime
subtitle: FBI UCR 1985–2016 + SPD NIBRS 2017–2026 · by neighborhood · real incident locations

hook.line: Seattle's reported crime, from the 1987 peak to 2007
hook.sub: FBI UCR · but the last five years tell a different story

punchline.sub: −48% under the same FBI measure · the plateau that followed is the next chapter

copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · SPD NIBRS
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports by MCPP neighborhood, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported offense location (block-level, sampled).
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 58 neighborhoods — every dot a real reported offense location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · by neighborhood, month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported offense location (block-level). Neighborhood detail begins 2017 — earlier records are counted, never guessed onto the map.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2016) and SPD NIBRS (2017+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line.
copy.sourceLine: Data: Seattle Police Department · data.seattle.gov (Public Domain) · some locations redacted
copy.coverageText: 98.4% of 2017+ records mapped · earlier years citywide
copy.creditsSources: Sources: Seattle Police Department Crime Data (2008–present, Public Domain) via data.seattle.gov & FBI UCR (1985–2016) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer. Neighborhood boundaries: Seattle MCPP (Micro-Community Policing Plans). Some incident locations are redacted by SPD; those records are counted but never plotted. Basemap (highways & landmarks): © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1987: the peak — 73,369 crimes reported in a single year.
1990: violent crime peaks at 7,780 reports — then begins a long decline.
By 2007 reports are down 48% from the 1987 peak — then the FBI measure ends.
The 2010s plateau: under SPD's broader count, reports hover near 80,000 a year.
2020 spikes to the highest single month on record — the last five years come next, up close.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported offense location — anonymized to the block by SPD. Redacted locations are counted, never plotted.
October 2021 — the busiest month of the last five years: 7,790 reports.
Capitol Hill has overtaken Downtown as Seattle's busiest area for Group A crime.
South Park: reported crime nearly halved since 2022 (−46%) — the city's biggest drop.
